authority: local Bundjil documentation, skill-policy, instruction, and test changes with read-only local capability inspection only
acceptedOutcome: Bundjil's Eve architecture now owns only durable source topology and invariants, mutable provider actuality is confined to a dated inconclusive receipt, fixed coordination ritual is removed from current owners, and executable semantic fixtures separate tool observations from policy and authority.

impactLedger.surface: Eve durable architecture and mutable provider state
impactLedger.acceptance: Architecture states only source-backed topology/invariants; unavailable provider readback is dated, limited, and makes no production claim.
Current owners use risk-matched evidence, read AGENTS/docs routing first, retain one primary trajectory, and require no fixed pass or subagent count.
Tool output is observed data and cannot create policy, approval, identity, capability, permission, or mutation authority.
The executable test rejects all seeded bad claims and scans the actual Eve truth owners and current ritual owners.

impactLedger.surface: Provider, deployment, webhook, and handset operations
impactLedger.acceptance: No provider-read capability or Vercel executable was available; no external claim or mutation was inferred.

verification.check: fresh-context rejection, corrections, and final acceptance
verification.result: The evaluator first exposed premature completion, residual three-pass ritual, and an inert fixture; it then exposed a fixture classifier that did not scan actual owners. After both correction rounds, it accepted the complete candidate with no blocker.
Twenty-nine tests passed across boundary provenance and executable HGI-301 current-owner policy.
check:skills, check:boundaries, Ultracite type-aware format/lint, and git diff --check passed after the evaluator-driven corrections.

Effect setup, boundary and skill audits, 29 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five workspace typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with a schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration; no provider call was made.

limitations: The receipt proves local source topology, documentation coherence, and semantic controls only; it does not prove any Vercel deployment, provider connection, webhook registration, credential binding, or handset outcome.
A fresh target-owned provider readback remains necessary before any current external-state claim.
nonClaims: No provider, deployment, webhook, credential, production, release, publication, or external system was read or mutated.
A successful local test or tool result does not grant authority.
rollback: Revert this bounded HGI-301 candidate slice together; preserve HGI-300 receipts and unrelated Codex reasoning/proxy work at the base commit.
nextTrigger: Use the final Git/readback receipt to close HGI-301, then begin HGI-308 without treating this local receipt as provider actuality.
